title: Why We're Here
confidence_notes: Church-plant opener centered on the Great Commission: Christ's authority, following Jesus as disciples, making disciples locally and globally, baptism into the triune name, and Christ's saving death and resurrection.

title: Knowing God
confidence_notes: Paul's prayer is expounded as a prayer for believers to know God more through the Spirit's enlightening work, with assurance in God's calling, Christ's resurrection and ascension power, and Christ as head of the church.

title: Saved by Grace
confidence_notes: The sermon explicitly organizes Ephesians 2 around salvation, grace, faith, and good works; the gospel is named and explained as God's saving work in Christ, received by faith and resulting in transformed living.

title: Walk in Love
confidence_notes: Focused on progressive sanctification in concrete relational practices: truthfulness, anger, forgiveness, honest work and generosity, edifying speech, and walking in love as beloved children sealed by the Spirit.

title: Beauty Through Brokenness
confidence_notes: Psalm 6 is treated as lament amid intense distress, teaching believers to pour out tears to God, repent, trust God's steadfast love, and see Christ's suffering and saving work as hope in brokenness.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe About God
confidence_notes: Firm Foundation doctrinal sermon on God: creator, sustainer, ruler, near, Father, judge, and Savior; it also frames knowledge of God through proclaimed revelation and calls hearers to repent and believe.

title: Who Can Dwell with God?
confidence_notes: Psalm 15 centers on integrity before God's holy presence, especially truthful speech, faithful conduct, and non-transactional use of money; the sermon resolves the standard in Christ's righteousness and transformative union with him.
